weekly_brief_narrative:
The dominant signal this week is the persistent EU AI Act Standards Vacuum, active under all three trigger conditions ahead of August 2026 high-risk compliance deadlines. No harmonised standards appear in the Official Journal despite CEN-CENELEC drafts in progress, creating compliance uncertainty for GPAI and high-risk systems. This elevates Layer 3 risks in the 7-layer tracker, with the AI Office focusing on delegated acts and GPAI Code finalisation.

Frontier capabilities remain stable with benchmark saturation; no new model releases from Anthropic, OpenAI, or peers, and self-reported benchmarks lack independent corroboration. Regulatory milestones hold steady: EU on track but vacuumed, US EO 14110 implemented without change (executive durability noted), UK ASI operational, China CAC rules enforced. Safety incidents absent across tiers; geopolitics quiet on US-China fronts; investments and deployments routine.

Framework highlights: EU Layer 4 GPAI Code advances transparency obligations (10^25 FLOP systemic threshold key), but Chinese labs trigger Ciyuan Vacuum via non-jurisdictional models. US actions remain revocable; UK pro-innovation; China opaque. Cross-monitor signals dormant—no FIMI ops, conflicts, or energy spikes.

Monitor in coming week: CEN-CENELEC standards tracker for Official Journal publications; AI Office supervisory opinions on GPAI; potential Q2 high-risk classification list; any lab compute disclosures nearing systemic thresholds. Thresholds include first Omnibus trilogue outcomes and NCA enforcement capacity reports.
